items.title: Anthropic is launching Claude Cowork on mobile and web
items.raw_content: Starting Tuesday, Anthropic's Claude Cowork AI platform will be available on mobile and web for the first time. The expanded access is rolling out first to Max subscribers and coming to Claude users on other plans "in the coming weeks." Claude Cowork was previously only accessible through the Claude desktop app for macOS and Windows, […]

items.title: AI chip maker SambaNova raises $1B at $11B valuation, 5 months after last mega round
items.raw_content: AI chip maker SambaNova has raised at an $11B valuation months after Intel was rumored to be trying to buy it for about $1.6 billion.
items.summary: AI chip maker SambaNova has raised at an $11B valuation months after Intel was rumored to be trying to buy it for about $1.6 billion.

items.title: Savi’s app aims to protect consumers from realistic AI scams like kidnappers demanding ransom
items.raw_content: The company just raised $7 million in seed funding, and is launching its app for iPhone and Android on Tuesday.
items.summary: The company just raised $7 million in seed funding, and is launching its app for iPhone and Android on Tuesday.

items.title: Meta’s new Muse Image model can pull other Instagram users into AI photos
items.raw_content: Meta is launching the first AI image generation model made by its Superintelligence Labs division. The Muse Image model now powers the image-making tools across the Meta AI app, Instagram, and WhatsApp, and it's coming soon to Facebook and Messenger, according to an announcement on Tuesday. It's part of the growing Muse family of AI […]
items.summary: Meta is launching the first AI image generation model made by its Superintelligence Labs division. The Muse Image model now powers the image-making tools across the Meta AI app, Instagram, and WhatsAp

items.title: Hot French startup ZML releases free product to speed inference across lots of AI chips
items.raw_content: ZML, a hot French AI startup endorsed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun, has now released ZML/LLMD, software that could make running AI less costly.
items.summary: ZML, a hot French AI startup endorsed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun, has now released ZML/LLMD, software that could make running AI less costly.

items.title: Why the rise of open source AI isn’t hurting Anthropic … yet
items.raw_content: Open source models’ success isn’t coming at the expense of frontier labs. Instead, they each seem to capture two phases of the same life cycle.
items.summary: Open source models’ success isn’t coming at the expense of frontier labs. Instead, they each seem to capture two phases of the same life cycle.

items.title: Meta just launched a new AI generator, Muse Image, and users are already pushing back over use of their photos
items.raw_content: The new image-generating model has numerous use cases, including advertising and decorating, and creator-based opportunities.
items.summary: The new image-generating model has numerous use cases, including advertising and decorating, and creator-based opportunities.

items.title: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
items.raw_content: With this update, users can start a task from their desk, get status updates on their phone, and pick up the finished output later — even if their laptop is closed.
items.summary: With this update, users can start a task from their desk, get status updates on their phone, and pick up the finished output later — even if their laptop is closed.

items.title: Solos debuts an even lighter version of its camera-less smart glasses
items.raw_content: Solos announced a new version of its AirGo smart glasses, one that forgoes cameras for a sleeker design and an AI assistant that relies on voice interactions. Last year's AirGo A5 weighed 36 to 40 grams depending on the frame style, but the new AirGo A6 weigh around 19 grams. Part of the weight savings […]

items.title: Anthropic wants to develop its own drugs
items.raw_content: At the event "The Briefing: AI for Science" earlier this week, Anthropic announced Claude Science, a new "AI workbench for scientists" that pulls fragmented tools and datasets into one environment, and generates figures and visuals. Anthropic, already dominating the industry with its popular coding tools and powerful AI models, framed the launch around what it […]

items.title: Microsoft joins AI cost-cutting trend by relying more on its own models
items.raw_content: Microsoft is the latest Silicon Valley giant to cut back on its AI spending.
items.summary: Microsoft is the latest Silicon Valley giant to cut back on its AI spending.

items.title: Discord admits AI moderation bug wrongfully banned users over harmless images
items.raw_content: The company confirmed that the issue had been affecting accounts since May, with an additional 200 users banned over the weekend before its team identified and fixed the problem.
items.summary: The company confirmed that the issue had been affecting accounts since May, with an additional 200 users banned over the weekend before its team identified and fixed the problem.

items.title: The first American autonomous ground vehicles are fighting in Ukraine
items.raw_content: Forterra has deployed more than 100 of its self-driving ATVs in conflict zones in Ukraine.
items.summary: Forterra has deployed more than 100 of its self-driving ATVs in conflict zones in Ukraine.

items.title: Infuriating Google commercial imagines the founding fathers embracing AI
items.raw_content: "Group project, but make it 1776." That's how a new commercial for Google Workspace opens. And things only get cringier from there. The clip imagines what it would be like if the founding fathers turned to Google's collaboration tools and Gemini to help them draft the Declaration of Independence. Ben Franklin texts Thomas Jefferson to […]

items.title: The ‘first’ AI-run ransomware attack still needed a human
items.raw_content: An AI agent carried out the technical execution of a real-world ransomware attack for the first known time, but new details show a human still chose the victim, set up the infrastructure, and supplied stolen credentials — meaning it wasn't quite the fully autonomous cybercrime debut that last week's headlines suggested.

items.title: The fanfiction community is at war with AI — and itself
items.raw_content: Over the past week, a new fanworks movement has kicked off, with the aim to root out authors using generative AI. But the detection methods being implemented are questionable, and any fanfic writer could be caught in the crossfire. Broad distaste around the use of Claude, ChatGPT, and other AI tools has long been a […]

items.title: Some of the nation’s rich are letting AI teach their kids
items.raw_content: Most Americans don't trust AI. It's proven that it doesn't know what safe toppings for pizza are. People don't even want to listen to AI music. But none of that matters for some of America's wealthy, who are turning to AI to teach their kids instead of traditional schools. Companies like Forge Prep and Alpha […]

items.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Midjourney’s medical scanner leaves many questions unanswered
items.raw_content: Midjourney has shown more of its futuristic medical scanner. It still hasn't shown much proof it works. The AI startup, best known for generating images, released a behind-the-scenes video of its dunk-tank ultrasound scanner, which it plans to deploy in spas and hopes will transform medicine with cheap, detailed, radiation-free imaging. items.title: I spy
items.raw_content: I've long argued that Hollywood has simultaneously set and ruined our expectations for smart glasses. items.title: Microsoft is laying off 4,800 employees
items.raw_content: A year after cutting around 9,100 employees, Microsoft is making further layoffs today as it begins its new financial year. The software maker is laying off around 4,800 employees today, approximately 2.1 percent of its workforce. Most of the employees affected by today's cuts are in Microsoft's commercial sales business or the company's Xbox division. […]
